raw_text:
ERROR   [TC-G-001] interface.f90:7 <-> header.h:2  callback signature
  CALLBACK_RETURN_TYPE: Fortran abstract interface declares REAL(c_float)
  return (4 bytes in XMM0[31:0]). C typedef expects 'double' return
  (8 bytes in XMM0[63:0]). Caller reads 8 bytes; high 32 bits are garbage.

ERROR   [TC-G-001] interface.f90:8 <-> header.h:2  callback param[0]
  CALLBACK_PARAM_TYPE: Fortran abstract interface param 'x' is REAL(c_float).
  C typedef passes 'double x'. Fortran callback reads 4 bytes; receives 8.
  Argument is 4 bytes of the double value — always wrong.
